output:
I’m locating the implementation for story `27-4-sideband-streaming-to-tui:quality` and the files it touched, then I’ll review the diff against correctness, security, architecture, and AC coverage.I found the story work as uncommitted changes in the current tree, concentrated in the workflow runner, gate/story machines, visualizer, and `run` command tests. Next I’m reading the story artifact plus the actual diff to check whether the code matches the intended sideband event flow.The story diff is mixed with follow-on review fixes. I’m narrowing in on the behavioral changes that affect sideband streaming and the workflow visualizer, because that’s where correctness and AC coverage risks will hide.I’ve identified a few nontrivial code paths in the review fixes, especially `snapshotToPosition()` and the new `dispatch-error` handling. I’m running focused validation now so the verdict reflects real behavior, not just static reading.I haven’t found a concrete defect yet, but AC 9 explicitly calls out the project test suite. I’m running the broader test commands now so the review can speak to AC coverage instead of assuming the earlier context still applies after these changes.The broader unit suite exposed one failure in `ink-renderer-lanes.test.ts`, specifically a 5s timeout in the performance test. I’m checking whether that’s a deterministic regression from these changes or a flaky suite issue before I decide whether it should count against the story.No actionable findings in the `27-4-sideband-streaming-to-tui:quality` implementation.

The sideband path is correct end to end: `dispatchTaskCore()` emits `dispatch-start` / `stream-event` / `dispatch-end`, `run.ts` bridges them into the renderer, story and gate machines now emit `dispatch-error` without mis-marking gate retries as failed stories, and `workflow-runner.ts` correctly filters inspect events to the root actor so child snapshots do not corrupt the workflow viz. I did not find a security issue or an architecture violation in story scope, and the AC coverage is reasonable for the behavior being added.

Validation I ran: `npm run build` passed, `npm run lint` passed, `npm test` passed (`47/47`), focused story-related vitest passed (`192` tests), and `src/lib/__tests__/ink-renderer-lanes.test.ts` passed on rerun (`25` tests). One full `npm run test:unit` run hit a timeout in the existing performance test at [src/lib/__tests__/ink-renderer-lanes.test.ts:484](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/ink-renderer-lanes.test.ts:484), but the isolated test and full file both reran cleanly, so I’m treating that as test flakiness rather than a defect in this story.

<verdict>pass</verdict>
<metrics tests-passed="264" tests-failed="0" lint-warnings="0" issues="0" />
